reasoning: The base rate of successfully appealing a Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) decision to the Swiss Federal Tribunal (SFT) is historically extremely low (around 0.3% to 1%). The SFT only entertains appeals on very narrow procedural grounds or human rights violations, not on the merits of the case. While Jordan Chiles and USA Gymnastics have filed multiple appeals and briefs as of early 2025, there has been no decision from the SFT as of November 2025. Reports from late 2025 state the situation is still "in limbo." With the resolution deadline of January 1, 2026 rapidly approaching (just over a month away), the probability of this highly unlikely successful appeal being resolved and announced within this timeframe is practically negligible. Therefore, I place the estimate at the minimum bound 0.02.
